From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Sloan Foundation](/w/index.php?title=Sloan_Foundation&redirect=no "Sloan Foundation"))

American philanthropic nonprofit organization

Alfred P. Sloan Foundation

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [Logo of the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation](/wiki/File:Alfred_P_Sloan_Foundation_Logo.png) | |
| Founded | 1934; 92 years ago (1934) |
| Founder | [Alfred Pritchard Sloan Jr.](/wiki/Alfred_P._Sloan_Jr. "Alfred P. Sloan Jr.") |
| Focus | Research and education in science, technology, engineering, mathematics and economics |
| Location | * [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), U.S. |
| Method | Grantmaking |
| Key people | Stacie Bloom (president)[[1]](#cite_note-bloom-pres-1) |
| [Endowment](/wiki/Financial_endowment "Financial endowment") | [US$](/wiki/United_States_dollar "United States dollar")2.0 [billion](/wiki/1000000000_(number) "1000000000 (number)") |
| Employees | 38[[2]](#cite_note-2) |
| Website | [sloan.org](https://sloan.org/) [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q1200258#P856 "Edit this at Wikidata") |

The **Alfred P. Sloan Foundation** is an American [philanthropic](/wiki/Philanthropy "Philanthropy") [nonprofit organization](/wiki/Nonprofit_organization "Nonprofit organization"). It was established in 1934 by [Alfred P. Sloan Jr.](/wiki/Alfred_P._Sloan "Alfred P. Sloan"), president and chief executive officer of [General Motors](/wiki/General_Motors "General Motors").

The Sloan Foundation makes grants to support original research and broad-based education related to science, technology, and economics. The foundation is an independent entity and has no formal relationship with General Motors.[[3]](#cite_note-about-3) As of 2024, the Sloan Foundation's assets totaled $2.2 billion.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alfred_P._Sloan_Foundation&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

During the initial years of [Alfred P. Sloan](/wiki/Alfred_P._Sloan "Alfred P. Sloan")'s presidency, the foundation devoted its resources almost exclusively to education in economics and business.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Grants were made to develop materials to improve high school and college economics teaching; for preparation of and wide distribution of inexpensive pamphlets on the pressing economic and social issues of the day; for weekly radio airing of round table discussions on current topics in economics and related subjects; and for establishing a Tax Institute at the [Wharton School](/wiki/Wharton_School_of_the_University_of_Pennsylvania "Wharton School of the University of Pennsylvania") of the [University of Pennsylvania](/wiki/University_of_Pennsylvania "University of Pennsylvania") to interpret new taxes and new trends in [public finance](/wiki/Public_finance "Public finance") for the average citizen.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

From 1936 to 1945, [Harold S. Sloan](/wiki/Harold_S._Sloan "Harold S. Sloan"), an economist and Alfred's younger brother, served as director and vice president of the foundation.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

The Sloan Foundation also made many civic contributions to the foundation's home city of New York, including grants to [Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts](/wiki/Lincoln_Center_for_the_Performing_Arts "Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts"), [Channel 13](/wiki/WNET "WNET"), [New York Public Library](/wiki/New_York_Public_Library "New York Public Library"), [New York University](/wiki/New_York_University "New York University"), and the Fund for the City of New York.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Starting in 2026, Stacie Bloom assumed presidency of the foundation.[[1]](#cite_note-bloom-pres-1)

## Programs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alfred_P._Sloan_Foundation&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Programs")]

The Alfred P. Sloan Foundation makes grants in seven broad subjects, known within the foundation as major program areas:

* Science,
* Economics,
* STEM Higher Education,
* Digital Information Technology,
* Public Understanding of Science, Technology & Economics,
* Working Longer,
* Energy & Environment.[[3]](#cite_note-about-3)

The Sloan Work and Family Researchers Network supports research and education about work-family issues. The foundation also funded the national workplace flexibility campaign[[10]](#cite_note-10) as part of the Working Families program led by [Kathleen E. Christensen](/wiki/Kathleen_E._Christensen "Kathleen E. Christensen").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

The [Sloan Research Fellowships](/wiki/Sloan_Research_Fellowship "Sloan Research Fellowship") are annual awards given to more than 126 young researchers and university faculty, to further studies in [chemistry](/wiki/Chemistry "Chemistry"), computational and evolutionary microbiology, [computer science](/wiki/Computer_science "Computer science"), [economics](/wiki/Economics "Economics"), [mathematics](/wiki/Mathematics "Mathematics"), [neuroscience](/wiki/Neuroscience "Neuroscience"), ocean sciences and [physics](/wiki/Physics "Physics").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In March 2008, the foundation awarded a $3 million grant to the [Wikimedia Foundation](/wiki/Wikimedia_Foundation "Wikimedia Foundation").[[13]](#cite_note-3mill2-13) It made additional grants in July 2011 and January 2017.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

The Sloan Foundation is the primary funder of the [Sloan Digital Sky Survey](/wiki/Sloan_Digital_Sky_Survey "Sloan Digital Sky Survey"), a major astronomical survey that began data collection in 2000.

In 1945, the Sloan Foundation donated $4 million to launch the Sloan Kettering Cancer Institute, now the Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

In 1950, the Sloan Foundation made a gift of more than $5 million to establish a School of Industrial Management, now known as the MIT Sloan School of Management.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

In 1955, the Sloan Foundation made a gift to establish the first two-year academic program in healthcare management in the US at Cornell University, initially called the Sloan Institute of Hospital Administration and now known as the Sloan Program in Health Administration.

## Presidents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alfred_P._Sloan_Foundation&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Presidents")]

1. [Alfred P. Sloan Jr.](/wiki/Alfred_P._Sloan_Jr. "Alfred P. Sloan Jr.") (1934–1961)
2. Everett Case (1962–1968)
3. [Nils Y. Wessell](/wiki/Nils_Yngve_Wessell "Nils Yngve Wessell")[[17]](#cite_note-17) (1969–1979)
4. [Albert Rees](/wiki/Albert_Rees "Albert Rees")[[18]](#cite_note-18) (1979–1988)
5. [Ralph E. Gomory](/wiki/Ralph_E._Gomory "Ralph E. Gomory") (1989–2007)
6. [Paul L. Joskow](/wiki/Paul_L._Joskow "Paul L. Joskow") (2008–2017)
7. [Adam F. Falk](/wiki/Adam_F._Falk "Adam F. Falk") (2018–2025)
8. Stacie Bloom (2025–)
